name: header
title: Header
description: Site header (banner landmark) with a site-title home link and a primary nav that collapses behind a native disclosure button below the md breakpoint. Nav content stays in the DOM (hidden attribute) so screen readers and find-in-page behave predictably. The skip link is not included: pages place the core .cui-skip-link anchor before the Header (and before the gov banner) as the first focusable element.
useWhen: The persistent top-of-page site identity and primary navigation, directly below the gov banner
Sites with a handful of top-level destinations that fit a single horizontal row on desktop
Sites serving multilingual audiences (every string is a prop, so the header can be fully localized per page)
avoidWhen: Application chrome with complex menus, mega-menus, or nested dropdowns (use the future navigation-menu component)
In-page or secondary navigation (use in-page navigation or side navigation instead)
More than once per page (one banner landmark per page)

keyboard: Tab moves through the title link, the menu button (below md), and the nav links in DOM order
Enter or Space on the menu button toggles the nav disclosure (native button semantics)
Escape anywhere inside the header closes the open menu and returns focus to the menu button
aria-expanded reflects the menu state and aria-controls references the nav landmark
Collapsed nav links are removed from the tab order via the hidden attribute (no focusable elements are hidden while reachable)
Links are standard: Enter activates; the current page carries aria-current="page" with a non-color indicator (2px block-end border + font weight)
